title: tetradymia axillaris var. axillaris
intro: Tetradymia axillaris var. axillaris, also known as Rock Mountain or Rock Hill Tetradymia, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain due to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The health benefits of Tetradymia axillaris var. axillaris include its potential to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
